questions.question: What is the best way to think of ChatGPT?
options: A search engine that finds information on the internet
A knowledgeable colleague who needs clear instructions to help effectively
A replacement for all human workers
A calculator that solves math problems

questions.question: Which ChatGPT version is recommended for beginners to start learning?
What makes a prompt effective?
options: Using as few words as possible
Including context, audience, tone, and constraints
Asking the same question multiple times

questions.question: A customer emails complaining about late delivery. Which prompt is BEST for writing a response?
options: Write a professional email response to a customer who received their order late. Apologize sincerely, explain shipping delays, and offer a 15% discount on next purchase. Tone: empathetic and solution-focused. 100-150 words.

questions.question: What is one common beginner mistake when using ChatGPT?
options: Treating it like Google and expecting real-time data

questions.question: What should you do if ChatGPT's first response isn't quite what you need?
options: Give up and write it yourself
Iterate and refine with follow-up prompts
Use the exact same prompt again

questions.question: How can ChatGPT help with meeting notes?
options: It can attend meetings for you
It can turn messy notes into structured summaries with action items
It cannot help with meetings at all

questions.question: What is the estimated time savings for using ChatGPT to write emails (according to the module)?
What does 'LLM' stand for in the context of ChatGPT?
Which scenario is ChatGPT BEST suited for (without plugins)?
options: Drafting a professional business proposal based on your instructions
Booking a flight for next week
